title: 10 best herbal tinctures for knee swelling

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric tinctures helps with knee swelling because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, specifically curcuminoids. These compounds work to reduce prostaglandins, hormone-like substances that cause inflammation and swelling in the joints. Additionally, turmeric's bioactive compounds have been shown to inhibit the production of enzymes that break down joint cartilage, helping to reduce joint damage and alleviate discomfort associated with knee swelling. As a result, turmeric tinctures can provide natural relief from knee pain and inflammation.
remedy_properties: Curcumin's anti-inflammatory effects: Turmeric contains curcumin, a powerful bioactive compound that inhibits the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, reducing knee swelling and pain.

Antioxidant properties: Turmeric's antioxidant compounds, such as vitamin C and beta-carotene, help neutralize free radicals that can contribute to inflammation and joint damage, promoting overall knee health.

Piperine-enhanced bioavailability: The tincture form of turmeric allows for enhanced bioavailability of curcumin due to the presence of piperine, a natural compound that increases curcumin absorption, thereby increasing its therapeutic effectiveness.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: It helps with knee swelling by inhibiting pro-inflammatory enzymes, such as COX-2 and 5-LOX, which are involved in the production of inflammatory mediators that contribute to swelling and pain.

Demethoxycurcumin: It exhibits anti-inflammatory properties by blocking the action of nuclear factor kappa B (NF-κB), a protein complex that promotes the expression of genes involved in inflammation and swelling.

Bisdemethoxycurcumin: It has potent anti-inflammatory effects by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, such as TNF-α and IL-1β, which are involved in the inflammatory response that leads to knee swelling.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Curcuma longa are used to make tinctures because they contain curcumin and other bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties.
Buds: The buds of Curcuma longa are used to make tinctures as they contain a high concentration of curcumin and other bioactive compounds that help to reduce inflammation and alleviate knee swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh curcuma longa roots in the early morning after the dew has dried completely.
Clean and dry the harvested roots in a low-temperature oven for 2 hours at 150 degrees fahrenheit.
Chop the dried roots into small pieces using a sharp knife and weigh out 1 part roots to 2 parts 95 percent ethanol.
Combine the chopped roots and ethanol in a clean glass jar and steep for 2-3 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth and discard the solids then bottle the tincture and store in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White willow tinctures helps with knee swelling because they contain salicin, a natural compound that has anti-inflammatory properties. This allows it to effectively reduce inflammation and pain associated with knee injuries or conditions such as osteoarthritis. As an alternative to traditional NSAIDs, white willow tincture can help alleviate joint stiffness and discomfort, promoting improved mobility and reduced swelling in the knees. Additionally, its natural composition makes it a gentle yet effective solution for managing chronic knee issues.
remedy_properties: Salicin content: White willow tincture contains salicin, a natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory compound that helps to reduce pain and swelling in the knees by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins, which are chemicals that cause inflammation.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in white willow tincture help to neutralize free radicals that can damage tissues and contribute to chronic inflammation, thereby reducing knee swelling and improving overall joint health.

Anti-inflammatory flavonoids: White willow tincture contains flavonoids like salicin and catechin, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce knee swelling by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and blocking the activation of inflammatory enzymes.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Flavonoids, particularly salicin, found in Salix alba tinctures, have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and pain in the knee by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.
Salicylic acid: Salicylic acid, a phenolic compound present in Salix alba, has potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, which help alleviate knee swelling by reducing inflammation and pain.

Tannins: Tannins, a class of polyphenolic compounds found in Salix alba, have astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce knee swelling by constricting blood vessels and reducing fluid accumulation in the affected area.
remedy_parts: Barks: The barks of Salix alba contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and pain in the knees.
Leaves: The leaves of Salix alba are rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help alleviate knee swelling.
Buds: The buds of Salix alba contain salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, which helps to reduce inflammation and relieve knee pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of salix alba bark and clean it thoroughly to remove dirt and debris.
Chop the bark into small pieces to increase its surface area for infusion.
Combine 1 cup of the chopped bark with 2 cups of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2 to 3 weeks to allow extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger tinctures helps with knee swelling because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce pain and inflammation in the joint. The bioactive compounds in ginger, such as gingerols and shogaols, have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory hormones, thereby reducing swelling and stiffness in the knees. Additionally, ginger's antioxidant properties may also help protect the knee joints from damage caused by free radicals, promoting overall joint health and function.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, help reduce swelling by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that cause inflammation.

Pain relief: The analgesic and anti-nociceptive properties of ginger tincture help alleviate pain associated with knee swelling, making it an effective natural remedy for relieving joint discomfort and stiffness.
Antioxidant activity: Ginger's antioxidant properties help protect the knee joints from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to inflammation and swelling.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These sesquiterpenoid compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce knee swelling by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.
Shogaols: These ginger-derived compounds have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects that help alleviate knee pain and swelling by blocking the action of pain-causing neurotransmitters.

Gingerol-6 (6-Gingerol) and Gingerol-8 (8-Gingerol): These two gingerols have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce knee swelling by inhibiting the activity of pro-inflammatory cytokines and scavenging free radicals.
remedy_parts: Buds: Zingiber officinale buds are used in tinctures due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds, including gingerols and shogaols, which contribute to their anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, making them suitable for treating knee swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh zingiber officinale rhizomes in the fall or early spring when the plants are dormant.
Clean and peel the rhizomes then chop them into small pieces approximately 1 inch in length.
Combine 1 part chopped rhizomes with 2 parts 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool dark place for 2 to 3 weeks shaking it daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Frankincense tinctures helps with knee swelling because they contain boswellic acids, which have potent anti-inflammatory properties that target the root cause of swelling. These acids inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, reducing inflammation and pain in the affected joint. Additionally, frankincense contains antioxidants that help to protect the joint tissues from further damage, promoting healing and reducing stiffness. By addressing the underlying causes of knee swelling, frankincense tinctures can provide relief and improve overall knee health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Frankincense tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as boswellic acids, help reduce knee swelling by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals and thereby reducing inflammation.

Analgesic: The analgesic properties of frankincense tincture, which include pain-relieving compounds like beta-boswellic acid, help alleviate knee pain associated with swelling by blocking pain signals to the brain.
Antioxidant: Frankincense tincture's antioxidant activity helps protect knees from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to inflammation and swelling.
remedy_constituents: Acetyl-11-keto-β-boswellic acid (AKBA): AKBA is a boswellic acid that inhibits the production of inflammatory mediators, thereby reducing swelling and pain in the knee joints.

Boswellic acids (especially β-boswellic acid): These acidic compounds have anti-inflammatory and anti-arthritic properties, which help reduce knee swelling by blocking the production of inflammatory enzymes and cytokines.

Terpenoids (especially 3-acetyl-11-keto-β-boswellic acid and β-boswellic acid): Terpenoids in Boswellia serrata tinctures exhibit anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, which may help alleviate knee swelling and associated pain by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory molecules.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Boswellia serrata contain bioactive compounds such as boswellic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate knee swelling.
Stems: The stems of Boswellia serrata contain resin, which is rich in boswellic acids and other anti-inflammatory compounds that contribute to the plant's therapeutic effects on knee swelling.
Barks: The barks of Boswellia serrata also contain boswellic acids and other bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them useful for treating knee swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh boswellia serrata resin from trees between 5-7 years old during the dry season.
Dry the harvested boswellia serrata resin in a single layer at 50-60 degrees celsius for 24 hours.
Grind 1 part of dried boswellia serrata resin with 2 parts of 80% ethanol in a glass container.
Steep the mixture for 2-3 weeks in a cool dark place with occasional shaking.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter and store the tincture in a dark glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Devil's claw tinctures helps with knee swelling because they contain a unique combination of compounds that work together to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain. The tincture's anti-inflammatory properties help to diminish excess fluid accumulation in the knee joint, thus reducing swelling. Additionally, devil's claw's analgesic effects provide relief from pain and discomfort associated with knee swelling, allowing for improved mobility and reduced stiffness. As a result, herbal devil's claw tinctures have become a popular natural remedy for managing knee health issues.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Devil's Claw contains anti-inflammatory compounds such as harpagosides, which help to reduce inflammation and pain in the joints, including knees, by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory hormones.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidant properties of Devil's Claw tinctures help to neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and damage to joint tissues, thereby reducing knee swelling and pain.

Analgesic and anodyne effects: Devil's Claw has been shown to have analgesic (pain-relieving) and anodyne (pain-soothing) effects, which can help to reduce knee pain and discomfort associated with swelling.
remedy_constituents: Harpagoside: This iridoid glycoside constituent has anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and pain in knee joints.
Procumbideside: Similar to harpagoside, this iridoid glycoside constituent is believed to contribute to the anti-inflammatory effects of Harpagophytum procumbens, helping alleviate knee swelling.

Sesquiterpenes: The sesquiterpene constituents in Harpagophytum procumbens tinctures may exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce swelling and promote overall joint health.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the primary part used for tinctures as they contain the highest concentration of harpagoside, a key compound responsible for anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
Stems: The stems of Harpagophytum procumbens are also used, although to a lesser extent, as they contain smaller amounts of harpagoside, which still provides therapeutic benefits.
Leaves: Some sources suggest that the leaves may be used in combination with roots and stems to create tinctures, although their harpagoside content is generally lower than the roots and stems.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1:5 ratio of fresh harpagophytum procumbens roots to menstruum such as glycerin or ethanol.
Chop the roots into small pieces to increase surface area for efficient extraction.
Combine chopped roots with menstruum in a clean glass jar and seal it.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks to allow for infusion.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ashwagandha tinctures helps with knee swelling because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The herb contains a group of compounds called withanolides, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain in joints. Additionally, ashwagandha's adaptogenic properties help to reduce stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for knee swelling. By reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation, ashwagandha tinctures can provide relief from knee swelling and discomfort, allowing individuals to enjoy greater mobility and comfort in their daily lives.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Ashwagandha tincture helps reduce inflammation in the knees by inhibiting pro-inflammatory enzymes and promoting anti-inflammatory compounds, thereby alleviating swelling and pain.

Oxidant scavenging activity: Ashwagandha's antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate knee inflammation and damage, reducing oxidative stress and promoting tissue healing.

Immunomodulatory effects: Ashwagandha tincture has been shown to modulate the immune system, which can help reduce excessive inflammation and swelling in the knees by regulating the activity of pro-inflammatory immune cells.
remedy_constituents: Withanolides: These alkaloids, particularly withaferin A, have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce knee swelling by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and enzymes.

Saponins: These triterpenoid glycosides have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can contribute to the reduction of knee swelling by modulating immune responses and protecting against oxidative stress.

Withanone: This withanolide has been shown to have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate knee swelling by reducing inflammation and oxidative damage in the affected area.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds like withanolides, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
Leaves: The leaves are another key part used in tinctures for knee swelling as they contain withanolides and other compounds that help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain.

Stems: The stems of Withania somnifera are also used in tincture production, as they contain a mixture of alkaloids and withanolides that contribute to their anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh or 300g of dried withania somnifera roots and leaves when they are in full bloom.
Chop the harvested material into small pieces to increase the surface area for extraction.
Combine the chopped plant material with 500ml of 40% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar occasionally.
Strain and filter the liquid tincture using cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove plant residue.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bell pepper tinctures helps with knee swelling because they contain powerful anti-inflammatory compounds that effectively reduce joint pain and inflammation. The bioactive ingredients in bell peppers, such as capsaicin and carotenoids, have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, thereby reducing swelling and stiffness in the knees. Additionally, the tinctures' antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate inflammation, promoting a faster recovery and improved mobility.
remedy_properties: Cicloxgenase inhibitors: Peppers contain compounds that inhibit the production of prostaglandins, which are involved in pain and inflammation pathways, potentially reducing knee swelling and discomfort.
Antioxidants: The high antioxidant content in peppers may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to knee swelling and arthritis symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Peppers contain capsaicinoids, such as capsaicin, which have anti-inflammatory properties that may help alleviate knee swelling and pain by reducing prostaglandins and leukotrienes production.
remedy_constituents: Capsaicin: Capsaicin helps with knee swelling by inhibiting the production of substance P, a neurotransmitter involved in pain signaling, thereby reducing inflammation and discomfort in the affected area.

Quercetin: Quercetin helps with knee swelling by acting as a potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compound, which reduces oxidative stress and inflammation in the joints, thereby alleviating swelling and pain.

Diosgenin: Diosgenin helps with knee swelling by reducing inflammation and inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, such as IL-1β and TNF-α, which contribute to joint inflammation and swelling.
remedy_parts: Fruits: Capsaicin-rich peppers from the Capsicum annuum plant are used to make tinctures for knee swelling due to capsaicin's anti-inflammatory properties.
Seeds: Capsicum annuum seeds are used to make tinctures for knee swelling due to their high concentration of capsaicin and other bioactive compounds.
Leaves: Capsicum annuum leaves are used to make tinctures for knee swelling due to their high content of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, including capsaicin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 pound of fresh capsicum annuum peppers when they are ripe and fully matured.
Chop the peppers into small pieces and combine them with 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the chopped peppers in the vodka for 2 weeks or 14 days in a cool dark place.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container.
Store the tincture in a cool dark place for 2 weeks or 14 days before using it.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort tinctures helps with knee swelling because it contains bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties. The herb has been traditionally used to alleviate joint pain and inflammation, and its active ingredients, such as hyperforin and adhyperforin, are believed to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory molecules. When taken as a tincture, these compounds can help reduce knee swelling by decreasing inflammation and promoting healing in the affected area, providing relief from discomfort and stiffness associated with swollen knees.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: St John's Wort tincture contains compounds that reduce inflammation, which helps alleviate knee swelling by decreasing the production of pro-inflammatory mediators and inhibiting the activation of inflammatory cells.

Antioxidant property: The antioxidants present in St John's Wort tincture help protect against oxidative stress and tissue damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to knee swelling by reducing inflammation and promoting healing.

Pain-relieving property: St John's Wort contains flavonoids that have analgesic and anxiolytic effects, helping to reduce pain associated with knee swelling, such as stiffness, tenderness, and discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Hyperforin: A phenolic compound that has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and pain in the knees by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.

Nortropane alkaloids (e.g. nortropane carboxylic acid): Bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, which can help alleviate knee swelling and pain by blocking pain pathways in the body.

Quercetin: A flavonoid with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce swelling in the knees by scavenging free radicals and inhibiting the production of inflammatory cytokines.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Known for their anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling in the knees.
Flowers: Rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which provide anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that can help alleviate knee swelling.
Roots: Contain bioactive compounds that exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them useful for treating knee swelling and pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of hypericum perforatum flowers and leaves on a sunny day.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm place for 1-2 weeks.
Combine the dried plant material with 60-80% ethanol in a 1:5 ratio by weight.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks or 2-3 weeks in the sun.
Strain and bottle the tincture after the steeping period and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Meadowsweet tinctures helps with knee swelling because of its unique ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain. The herb contains salicylates, which are similar in structure to aspirin, making it an effective natural alternative for managing arthritic symptoms. When taken as a tincture, meadowsweet's anti-inflammatory properties work to decrease the production of prostaglandins, which contribute to swelling and joint discomfort, providing relief from knee stiffness and pain. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to soothe sore joints and promote overall comfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Meadowsweet contains salicylates, which are natural pain relievers that help reduce inflammation and alleviate swelling in the knees.

Antioxidant activity: The tannins present in meadowsweet have antioxidant properties, which help protect the joints from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, reducing knee swelling and discomfort.

Analgesic and antipyretic effects: Meadowsweet's salicylic acid content also exhibits analgesic (pain-relieving) and antipyretic (fever-reducing) properties, providing relief from joint pain and swelling in the knees.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Filipendula ulmaria is rich in flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and pain in the knees.

Phenolic acids: The phenolic acids present in Filipendula ulmaria, including ferulic and caffeic acids, have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that may help to alleviate knee swelling and promote healing.

Triterpenoids: Filipendula ulmaria contains triterpenoids like ursolic acid, which has been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and anti-arthritic properties that may help to reduce knee swelling and improve joint mobility.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used due to their high concentration of salicylic acid, a compound similar to aspirin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and pain in the knees.
Leaves: The leaves are used for their diuretic properties, which can help reduce fluid retention and alleviate swelling in the knees.
Flowers: The flowers are used due to their anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help relax muscles and reduce inflammation and pain in the knees.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of fresh or dried filipendula ulmaria flowers by cutting them above the node.
Chop 2 parts of vodka with 1 part of filipendula ulmaria flowers in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks shaking daily.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids.
Label and store the tincture in a cool dark place for up to 5 years.

The saponins present in marshmallow root bind to the mucous membranes, soothing inflamed areas and reducing fluid buildup. Anti-inflammatory compounds: The tannins and flavonoids present in marshmallow tinctures have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals that contribute to knee swelling.

remedy_constituents: Mucilages: Mucilages help to reduce inflammation and swelling by forming a protective barrier on the surface of the skin, allowing the affected area to heal and recover.

Triterpenoid Saponins: Triterpenoid saponins exhibit anti-inflammatory and anti-arthritic properties, helping to reduce knee swelling by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines.

Iridoid Glycosides: Iridoid glycosides have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce knee swelling by suppressing the production of pro-inflammatory mediators and protecting against oxidative stress.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Althaea officinalis are primarily used due to their high mucilage content, which helps reduce inflammation and swelling.
Leaves: The leaves are used for their anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and calm swollen joints.
Barks: The barks are used for their ability to reduce inflammation and promote healing, making them a valuable component in tinctures for knee swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of fresh roots of althaea officinalis in the morning after dew has evaporated.
Chop 1 part of the harvested root into small pieces to increase surface area for extraction.
Combine the chopped root with 2-3 parts of 80% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 2-6 weeks in a cool dark place with occasional shaking.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion tinctures helps with knee swelling because they contain bioactive compounds that have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The tincture's ability to reduce inflammation in the joints can help alleviate symptoms of arthritis, such as pain and stiffness, allowing for improved mobility and reduced swelling. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers in the morning after dew has dried for optimal potency.
Chop the harvested plant material into small pieces and transfer it to a clean glass jar filled to 90% capacity.
Add 50% of the jar's volume with high-proof vodka and stir gently to ensure all plant material is submerged.
Store the mixture in a dark, cool place for six weeks while allowing it to macerate and extract active compounds.
Strain the liquid through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into another glass container and discard the solids after one week of sedimentation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Slippery elm tinctures helps with knee swelling because of its unique ability to soothe and calm inflamed tissues. remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry high-quality ulmus rubra leaves in a warm and well-ventilated area for at least two weeks.
Combine one part dried ulmus rubra leaves with three parts 95% ethanol vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for four to six weeks, shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain the tincture through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids after one hour of pressing.
Store the filtered tincture in a cool dark place and allow it to mature for at least two months before using, with a final dosage of 30 drops three times daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian tinctures helps with knee swelling because it has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce joint pain and discomfort. Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 6-8 weeks to allow for slow extraction and infusion.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another clean glass bottle for consumption.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried epilobium angustifolium flowers according to the recommended dosage of one part herbs to five parts vodka.
Chop the harvested herbs into small pieces to increase surface area and facilitate extraction.
Combine the chopped herbs with vodka in a clean glass jar at a ratio of 1:5, ensuring the herbs are completely submerged.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for four weeks, shaking the jar daily to enhance extraction and ensure even distribution.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another container, discarding the solids and preserving the liquid for use as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage tinctures helps with knee swelling because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively reduce pain and stiffness in the joints. Additionally, sage's antioxidant properties help to protect the knees from further damage, promoting healthy joint function and flexibility. By reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, sage tinctures provide a natural and effective way to manage knee swelling and alleviate related discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Sage tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with knee swelling.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in sage tincture help protect the knees from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to joint inflammation and swelling.

Anti-spasmodic properties: Sage tincture's anti-spasmodic compounds relax muscles and reduce muscle spasms, which can help alleviate knee pain and swelling by reducing tension and stiffness in the surrounding tissues.
remedy_parts: Roots: Sage roots have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing knee swelling and pain.
Rhyzomes: The rhizome, which is the underground stem of the sage plant, contains compounds that help reduce inflammation and relieve knee swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried leaves of salvia officinalis in the morning after dew has evaporated for maximum potency.
Measure out 200g of dried salvia officinalis leaves and transfer them to a clean glass jar for infusion.
Combine the 200g of dried salvia officinalis leaves with 500ml of high-proof vodka and seal the jar tightly for maceration.
Store the mixture in a dark cupboard at room temperature for 14 days to allow for maximum extraction of active compounds.
Strain the tincture through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and labeling the tincture with date and ingredients used.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mojave yucca tinctures helps with knee swelling because of its unique blend of bioactive compounds that target inflammation and pain. The saponins present in Mojave yucca work by reducing the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals, thereby alleviating swelling and discomfort. Additionally, the tincture's antioxidant properties help to repair damaged tissues, promoting healthy joint function and flexibility. As a natural anti-inflammatory agent, Mojave yucca tinctures provide effective relief from knee swelling, making it an excellent alternative to conventional painkillers and medications.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Mojave Yucca contains bioactive compounds like saponins, flavonoids, and polyphenols, which have been shown to exhibit potent anti-inflammatory activity, helping to reduce knee swelling by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.

Pain-relieving properties: The tincture's analgesic properties can help alleviate knee pain and discomfort associated with swelling, making it a natural alternative to NSAIDs (non-steroidal anti-inflammatory drugs).

Antioxidant and immune-modulating effects: Mojave Yucca's antioxidant properties help protect against oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, while its immunomodulatory effects can support the body's natural response to injury or inflammation, promoting healing and reducing knee swelling.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots have been traditionally used to reduce inflammation and relieve pain associated with knee swelling due to their anti-inflammatory compounds.
Barks: The barks contain a type of saponin that helps to reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort in the knees.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh yucca schidigera roots in the morning after dew has evaporated ensuring optimal potency.
Clean and chop the harvested roots into small pieces removing any dirt or debris carefully.
Combine 500g of chopped roots with 1000ml of vodka or grain alcohol in a glass jar leaving minimal headspace.
Steep the mixture for 6 weeks shaking the jar daily to ensure uniform extraction ensuring optimal tincture strength.
Strain the tincture through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into a clean dark glass bottle discarding solids completely.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Black cumin tinctures helps with knee swelling because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively reduce pain and discomfort associated with swollen knees. The tincture's active compound, thymoquinone, has been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, thereby decreasing inflammation and edema in the joints. Additionally, black cumin's natural antioxidant properties help protect the knee joint from oxidative stress, promoting healthy joint function and reducing swelling. Regular use of herbal black cumin tinctures can provide significant relief for individuals experiencing knee swelling due to various underlying conditions.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Black cumin's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce inflammation and swelling in the knee joint, providing relief from pain and discomfort.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant compounds present in black cumin tincture neutralize free radicals that can damage tissues and exacerbate swelling, promoting overall tissue health and reducing oxidative stress.
Pain-relieving: Black cumin's analgesic properties help alleviate knee joint pain and reduce discomfort associated with swelling, making it easier to manage symptoms and improve quality of life.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the black cumin plant contain a high concentration of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing knee swelling.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes have been traditionally used to treat joint pain and inflammation, including knee swelling, due to their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh nigella sativa seeds and dry them for at least 7 days in a single layer on paper towels.
Grind dried nigella sativa seeds into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for approximately 30 minutes.
Combine 10 grams of ground nigella sativa with 100 milliliters of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar, sealing it tightly for 14 days.
Strain the tincture mixture through cheesecloth into another clean glass jar, squeezing and twisting the cloth to extract every drop for 15 minutes.
Store the finished nigella sativa tincture in an amber glass bottle at room temperature, taking 20-30 drops three times daily as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tinctures helps with knee swelling because it contains a unique combination of compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties. The tannins in licorice root help to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain, while the glycyrrhizin component has been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes. Additionally, the saponins in licorice root have been found to have analgesic and antioxidant effects, which can further contribute to its anti-swelling properties. By targeting multiple pathways involved in inflammation, herbal licorice tinctures can provide effective relief from knee swelling.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Licorice root contains compounds like glycyrrhizin and licoricidin, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidant compounds in licorice tinctures help neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and damage to joint tissues, reducing swelling and promoting healing.

Immune-modulating properties: Licorice root has been traditionally used to modulate the immune response, which can help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of knee swelling by regulating the production of cytokines and other pro-inflammatory molecules.
remedy_parts: Roots: Licorice roots contain compounds that help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain in the knees, making them effective for treating knee swelling.
Barks: The barks of the licorice plant contain glycosides that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe and calm swollen knees.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots and grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 2 minutes.
Combine 100 grams of the powdered root with 500 milliliters of 95% ethanol in a clean glass jar, seal it tightly, and shake vigorously for 5 minutes.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 14 days, shaking the jar twice daily to facilitate extraction of bioactive compounds from the roots.
After 14 days, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids and measuring the resulting tincture's strength using a hydrometer at 20% abv.
Transfer the prepared tincture to dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids, store them in a cool dark place, and use within 2 years or after 1 year if stored in the refrigerator.

intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for knee swelling are concentrated plant extracts used to alleviate swelling and discomfort in the knee joints. They help by reducing inflammation, improving circulation, and soothing pain. The best herbal tinctures for knee swelling include Hamamelis virginiana, Arnica montana, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Urtica dioica, Vaccinium macrocarpon, Equisetum arvense, Echinacea purpurea, Rhus toxicodendron, Calendula officinalis, and Boswellia serrata. If left untreated, knee swelling can hinder daily activities, causing fatigue, limited mobility, and decreased productivity, ultimately affecting one's quality of life and overall well-being.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to knee swelling that are treated with herbal tinctures are conditions affecting joints, such as arthritis, gout, and rheumatism. Herbal tinctures like turmeric, ginger, and willow bark have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce pain and inflammation in the joints. Other ailments, including sprains, strains, and tendonitis, can also be treated with herbal tinctures rich in antioxidants and natural analgesics.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal tinctures that help with knee swelling is a blend of Turmeric, Ginger, and St. John's Wort. Turmeric contains curcumin, which reduces inflammation and pain. Ginger's anti-inflammatory properties also alleviate swelling and discomfort. St. John's Wort enhances blood circulation, promoting healing and reducing fluid retention. This synergistic combination can be taken in equal parts, 10-20 drops each, 2-3 times a day, mixed with water or a carrier oil. Consult a healthcare professional before using these tinctures, especially if you're taking medications.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Phytomedicine: international journal of phytotherapy and phytopharmacology, herbal remedies have been found to be effective in reducing inflammation and enhancing the healing process of soft tissue injuries. In this study, 16 rats received surgical transection to their right medial collateral ligaments (MCLs), and 8 of them were treated with an adhesive herbal plaster application to their right knees, while the other 8 were treated with plain adhesive plaster. The results showed that the ultimate tensile strength (UTS) and stiffness of the MCLs in the herbal remedy group were significantly larger than those in the control group at both 3 and 6 weeks post-operation, indicating that herbal remedies can improve the mechanical strength of healing tissues and reduce knee swelling.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hamamelis virginiana tinctures helps with knee swelling because it has anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce inflammation and ease pain in the affected joints. The tannins present in the plant also constrict blood vessels, thereby reducing blood flow to the area and minimizing swelling. Additionally, Hamamelis virginiana has a soothing effect on the skin, promoting relaxation and reducing discomfort associated with knee swelling. As a result, it can provide relief and promote faster recovery from knee swelling and associated discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Hamamelin: Hamamelin, a phenolic compound, helps reduce inflammation and alleviate knee swelling by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and mediators.
Hamamelose: Hamamelose, a polyol, has anti-inflammatory properties that contribute to the relief of knee swelling by reducing swelling and pain caused by inflammation.
Vasopressin: Vasopressin, a hormone-like compound, helps reduce knee swelling by constricting blood vessels and improving lymphatic drainage, thus reducing fluid accumulation in the knee area.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Hamamelis virginiana leaves are commonly used to make tinctures for knee swelling due to their anti-inflammatory properties.
Barks: Hamamelis virginiana barks are used to make tinctures for knee swelling due to their ability to reduce inflammation and relieve pain.
Roots: Hamamelis virginiana roots are used to make tinctures for knee swelling due to their high concentration of anti-inflammatory compounds.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 to 2 pounds of fresh hamamelis virginiana leaves and twigs in early summer when they are most potent.
Chop the harvested plant material into small pieces to increase the surface area for extraction.
Combine the chopped plant material with 80 to 100 proof vodka in a 1:3 ratio by weight in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 4 to 6 weeks, shaking it daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Arnica montana tinctures helps with knee swelling because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The herb's active compounds, such as sesquiterpene lactones, work to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain in the affected area. By inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, Arnica montana tinctures help to decrease swelling and promote healing. Additionally, its analgesic properties provide relief from discomfort and stiffness, making it a popular natural remedy for knee swelling and joint pain. Regular use may also improve overall joint health and function.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds help reduce inflammation and swelling in the knee by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines.

Alkanes and sesquiterpenes (including Helenalin and 11-epi-arnabin-7-alpha-ol): These terpenoid compounds have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help reduce pain and swelling in the knee by blocking the release of pain-causing chemicals.
Saponins: These glycosidic compounds help reduce inflammation and swelling in the knee by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory mediators and modulating the immune response.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of sesquiterpene lactones, which are responsible for the plant's anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: Leaves are also used as they contain similar compounds to the flowers, which help in reducing inflammation and swelling.
Roots: Roots are used to some extent as they contain glycosides, which may contribute to the plant's anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of fresh arnica montana flowers and leaves for the desired amount of tincture.
Chop the harvested plant material into small pieces and allow it to steep in 5 parts of 80 proof vodka for 2-6 weeks.
Strain the tincture mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle to remove plant material.
Store the tincture in a cool dark place for 2-4 weeks to allow the flavors to mature and the ethanol to fully extract the plant's properties.
Shake the tincture regularly and then bottle it in small dark glass bottles with a dropper cap for later use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra tinctures helps with knee swelling because it contains anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce inflammation and pain in the affected area. The tannins and saponins present in the tincture have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, which contribute to swelling and discomfort. Additionally, the tincture's ability to enhance the body's natural cortisol production can aid in reducing fluid retention, further alleviating knee swelling. This natural approach to managing knee swelling offers a promising alternative to pharmaceutical interventions.
remedy_constituents: Trigonelline: This alkaloid has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and pain in the knees.
Glycyrrhizin: This triterpenoid saponin has potent anti-inflammatory effects, which can help alleviate knee swelling and joint pain.
Licoricidol: This flavonoid has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the knees, leading to reduced swelling.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used because they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and anti-arthritic properties, which can help reduce knee swelling.
Leaves: The leaves are used because they contain a smaller amount of glycyrrhizin, but still have some anti-inflammatory properties, making them a suitable alternative to the roots.
Barks: The barks are used because they also contain glycyrrhizin and other compounds that may help reduce inflammation and alleviate knee swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50-100 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots, which should be rich in color and free from contamination.
Chop the roots into small pieces to increase their surface area for better extraction.
Combine 250ml of 70-80% ethanol with the chopped roots in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass container and discard the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Urtica dioica tinctures helps with knee swelling because they contain anti-inflammatory compounds such as isothiocyanates and saponins. These bioactive agents have been shown to reduce swelling and ease pain in the joints, particularly in the knees. By inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals and promoting the breakdown of excess fluids, Urtica dioica tinctures can provide relief from the discomfort and stiffness associated with knee swelling, promoting a sense of comfort and mobility in those affected. This natural remedy offers a promising solution for those seeking alternative solutions.
remedy_constituents: Sterols: Help reduce knee swelling by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory eicosanoids, which contribute to swelling and pain.
Polyphenols: Act as antioxidants, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the affected area, which can help alleviate knee swelling and discomfort.
Saponins: Exert anti-inflammatory effects by inhibiting the activity of pro-inflammatory enzymes, such as cyclooxygenase (COX), and reducing the production of inflammatory mediators.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Urtica dioica leaves are commonly used to make tinctures for knee swelling due to their high content of anti-inflammatory compounds and flavonoids.
Stems: The stems of Urtica dioica are used to make tinctures for knee swelling due to their rich supply of iridoid glycosides, which have anti-inflammatory properties.
Roots: Urtica dioica roots are used to make tinctures for knee swelling due to their high content of stigmasterol, a plant sterol with anti-inflammatory effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the fresh or dried leaves and stems of urtica dioica in the morning to ensure highest potency.
Chop the harvested urtica dioica plant material into small pieces to release its active compounds.
Mix one part of chopped urtica dioica plant material with two parts of 80% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks or 4-6 weeks for a stronger tincture.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle to remove the plant material.

remedies.remedy_desc: Vaccinium macrocarpon tinctures helps with knee swelling because of its anti-inflammatory properties and antioxidants. The plant, also known as cranberry, contains compounds that reduce inflammation and pain in the joints. The tannins and flavonoids present in Vaccinium macrocarpon work to combat oxidative stress, which contributes to swelling and discomfort. Additionally, the tincture may help to improve circulation and reduce fluid buildup, providing relief from knee swelling and promoting overall joint health. This natural remedy offers a potential solution for those seeking a holistic approach to managing knee swelling.
remedy_constituents: Quercetin: A flavonoid with anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation and swelling in the knee joint.
Furanocoumarin (e.g. umbelliferone): A phenolic compound with analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects that can help alleviate pain and swelling in the knee.
Proanthocyanidin (PAC): A type of phenolic compound with strong anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce inflammation and swelling in the knee joint.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are used due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate swelling.
Barks: Barks are used due to their potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can aid in reducing swelling and promoting healing.
Fruits: Fruits are used due to their high content of anthocyanins and other compounds with anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and promote overall health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of vaccinium macrocarpon berries in late summer or early fall when ripe and fully colored.
Dry the berries in a single layer at 95°f for 6 hours to remove excess moisture.
Combine 1 part dried vaccinium macrocarpon with 2 parts 95% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to macerate for 2-3 weeks in a cool dark place with occasional shaking.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth and discard the solids then bottle the tincture for storage.

remedies.remedy_desc: Equisetum arvense tinctures helps with knee swelling because of its anti-inflammatory properties. It contains caffeic acid and other compounds that have been shown to reduce inflammation and ease pain in the affected area. The tincture is also believed to improve blood circulation, which can help to reduce swelling and promote healing in the knee joint. remedy_constituents: Furanosesquiterpenes: These compounds help reduce inflammation and swelling in the knee by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.
Furanosesquiterpene glycosides: These compounds exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, which help alleviate knee swelling by reducing the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines.
Sesquiterpenes: These compounds have been found to possess anti-inflammatory and anti-arthritic properties, which help reduce knee swelling and alleviate joint pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Equisetum arvense roots are used to make tinctures for knee swelling due to their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
Stems: The stems of Equisetum arvense are used to make tinctures for knee swelling because of their ability to reduce inflammation and promote healing.
Leaves: Equisetum arvense leaves are used to make tinctures for knee swelling due to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and pain.
remedy_recipe: Gather 25-50 grams of dried equisetum arvense and clean it thoroughly with a soft brush.
Combine the cleaned plant material with 500 milliliters of 40% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool dark place for 2-4 weeks shaking occasionally every day.
Strain the liquid mixture through a cheesecloth into another clean glass container discarding the solids.
Filter the tincture through a coffee filter into a dark glass bottle and store it in a cool dark place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea tinctures helps with knee swelling because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which are believed to reduce swelling and ease pain in the joints. The tincture's ability to boost the immune system and reduce oxidative stress may also contribute to its beneficial effects on knee swelling. Additionally, Echinacea purpurea contains compounds like alkylamides and caffeic acid, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making it a popular natural remedy for joint health and inflammation.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds may help reduce knee swelling by inhibiting the production of inflammatory mediators and suppressing the activity of pro-inflammatory enzymes.

Caffeic acid derivatives: These phenolic compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate knee swelling by reducing the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and inhibiting the activity of enzymes involved in inflammation.

Chlorogenic acid: This polyphenol may help reduce knee swelling by scavenging free radicals, modulating the activity of inflammatory cells, and inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory mediators.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in inulin and polysaccharides, which may help reduce inflammation and swelling.
Flowers: Contain flavonoids and alkylamides that may help reduce pain and inflammation in the joints.
Leaves: Contain phenolic acids and other compounds that may help alleviate pain and inflammation associated with knee swelling.
remedy_recipe: Gather 100g of dried echinacea purpurea flowers roots and leaves from a reputable herbal supplier.
Combine the dried plant material with 500ml of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks shaking it daily to steep the mixture.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids.
Label and date the tincture then store it in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rhus toxicodendron tinctures helps with knee swelling because it targets the root cause of inflammation, which is often a result of past traumas or stress to the knee joint. The tincture's homeopathic properties stimulate the body's natural response to heal and repair damaged tissues, reducing inflammation and swelling. Additionally, Rhus toxicodendron tinctures can help to relax the muscles surrounding the knee joint, allowing for improved mobility and flexibility, which can contribute to a reduction in swelling and discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Anacardic acids: Help reduce inflammation by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and preventing the release of histamine, thereby alleviating knee swelling.
Phenolic compounds (including tannins): Act as anti-inflammatory agents by scavenging free radicals and reducing oxidative stress, which contribute to knee swelling and discomfort.
Sapogenins (including cardol and anacardol): Display analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce pain and swelling in the knees by modulating the body's inflammatory response.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Known for their anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties, which could help alleviate knee swelling.
Barks: May be used due to their potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Roots: Possibly used because of their alleged anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the rhus toxicodendron plant parts preferably in the fall when the sap is flowing freely outdoors.
Clean and dry the rhus toxicodendron plant parts for 2-3 days to prevent any mold growth.
Combine 1 part of the dried rhus toxicodendron plant parts with 2 parts of 95% grain alcohol in a clean glass container.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 6-8 weeks to allow the tincture to extract.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle for storage.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis tinctures helps with knee swelling because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The tincture is derived from the flowers of the Calendula plant, which contains compounds like triterpenoids and flavonoids. These compounds work to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the affected area. By decreasing inflammation, Calendula tinctures can alleviate swelling and pain in the knees, improving mobility and comfort. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to soothe and calm irritated tissues, making it a popular choice for knee swelling relief.
remedy_constituents: Sesquiterpenes: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and ease pain in the knees.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids in Calendula officinalis possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help mitigate knee swelling and promote healing.
Naphthoquinones: These compounds exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help reduce knee swelling and combat underlying infections that may contribute to knee inflammation.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are the primary part used due to their high concentration of anti-inflammatory compounds and flavonoids, which are effective in reducing knee swelling.
Leaves: They contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help in reducing inflammation and pain associated with knee swelling.
Stems: They are used in combination with flowers and leaves to create a balanced tincture, as they may contain some anti-inflammatory properties that complement the other parts.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 part calendula flowers and add to 2 parts 95% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle, pressing on the solids to extract.
Discard the solids and add a small amount of glycerin to the tincture to preserve it.
Label and store the finished tincture in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Boswellia serrata tinctures helps with knee swelling because its anti-inflammatory properties reduce inflammation and pain in the joints. The boswellic acids present in the tincture inhibit the production of inflammatory enzymes, which contribute to swelling and discomfort. By alleviating inflammation, Boswellia serrata tinctures provide relief from knee swelling and promote joint mobility. Additionally, its analgesic properties help to reduce pain, allowing individuals to move freely and engage in activities with greater ease and comfort.
remedy_constituents: Acetyl-11-keto-β-boswellic acid (AKBA): It has potent anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce knee swelling by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines.
α-boswellic acid (α-BA): It has anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, contributing to reduced knee swelling by suppressing the activity of pro-inflammatory cells and enzymes.

β-boswellic acid (β-BA): It exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to alleviate knee swelling by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory mediators and reducing oxidative stress.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain the highest concentration of boswellic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties.
Rhyzomes: Similar to roots, rhyzomes have a high boswellic acid content, which contributes to their anti-inflammatory effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh boswellia serrata resin, preferably from mature trees, and clean it thoroughly with water.
Weigh out 500 grams of the cleaned resin and add 1 liter of 95 percent ethanol to the container.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 3 weeks, shaking the container daily.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a separate container, then discard the solids.
Transfer the resulting tincture to dark glass bottles and store them in a cool dark place for up to 5 years.
